items.title: Arrested man a relative of Canberra stabbing victim, police say

children.content: Paramedics treated the woman for critical injuries at the scene but she was declared dead on her way to hospital.

image.altText: A residential street, police tape, and crime scene

items.title: 'Foldable' pistols, 3D-printed guns seized in raid 450m from police station

children.content: A police investigation has uncovered a large cache of weapons in the national capital's suburbs, less than a kilometre from the Belconnen Police Station.

image.altText: A long line of yellow, purple and white 3D-printed firearms.

items.title: CIT chief told she could be fired for disclosing misconduct probe

children.content: Margot McNeill did not tell the Canberra Institute of Technology board she was under investigation for misconduct when she was being recruited.

items.title: Long-awaited RSPCA facility given funding boost in federal budget

children.content: Other funding boosts for the ACT revealed in the budget papers include $50 million to duplicate Drake Brockman Drive in West Belconnen, aimed at servicing the area's growing population, and almost $10 million for the National Film and Sound Archive.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses laughs at the camera while holding a dog

items.title: Taser-death inquest told officers had 'plenty of time' to deal with Cooma 95yo

children.content: A senior NSW Ambulance officer tells the Clare Nowland inquest that emergency services could have taken more time responding to the 95-year-old's dementia-related aggression.

image.altText: an elderly woman looking at the camera

items.title: NSW South Coast residents fear homes could be lost to sea in next storm

children.content: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

image.altText: A photo of a dune

items.title: Canberra teen charged with planning terrorist attack

children.content: The 17-year-old appeared in the ACT Children's Court today charged with one count of planning or preparing for a terrorist act and one count of transmitting violent extremist material.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: Technicolour frogs released into the wild near Canberra to bolster numbers

children.content: More than 800 tiny, technicoloured northern corroboree frogs are released into Brindabella National Park to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

gallery.altText: An up close of the frogs, with black and yellow markings, in Del Leong's palm.
An up close of the Northern Corroboree frog, with black and yellow markings, in an enclosure.
A tiny black and yellow frog released into the wild, grass.

items.title: The key takeaways from Trump and Xi's meeting in China

children.content: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.

items.title: ISIS brides' arrest in Australia prompts anger in Syrian camp

children.content: The arrest of three Australian women linked to the Islamic State group sparks anger and frustration inside the Syrian refugee camp where they had lived for years.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

items.title: Who is in Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?

children.content: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.

image.altText: Trump and Xi walk along a red carpet in front of rows of delegates standing on stone stairs.

items.title: Power to cut funding and stricter 'permanence' test under new NDIS

children.content: Legislation designed to save $35 billion in NDIS spending is introduced to parliament, revealing new powers for the minister to cut funding to classes of supports, automate some decisions and create requirements for prospective participants to exhaust all other treatment options before entering the NDIS.

image.altText: A close-up profile image of a man with short hair, wearing a suit. The background is dark.

items.title: One dead and many injured in bus rollover on Queensland's Bruce Highway

children.content: Two passengers suffered life-threatening injuries after the large coach overturned between Townsville and Bowen.

image.altText: A green bus on its side in grass after a crash. Emergency service workers are seen on the right hand side.

items.title: Green light for Andrew Forrest's $70m Ningaloo Reef resort

children.content: After almost a decade of planning, mining billionaire Andrew Forrest's plan to build an eco-resort on the shores of the Ningaloo Reef has received the go-ahead.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

image.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.

items.title: Lions welcome Cats to the Gabba for grand final rematch

children.content: Brisbane and Geelong meet for the first time since the 2025 grand final, as the Lions welcome a Cats side out for revenge to the Gabba.

items.title: Maroons face must-win Origin clash at Lang Park

items.title: Blockbuster movies are connecting new audiences to classical music

children.content: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

items.title: Prime minister concedes aged care wait times 'not good enough'

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

image.altText: Albanese looking down in a suit while touching his ear.

items.title: Mouse plague so bad locals say driving feels like 'popping bubble wrap'

children.content: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.

items.title: Australian hantavirus ship passengers to depart for home today

children.content: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak are testing negative for the illness and are symptom-free, the federal health minister says, as they prepare to be repatriated to Australia.

image.altText: A plane on the tarmac at the airport surrounded by people in white body suits carrying white bags.

items.title: Antarctic expeditioner removed after weapon incident

children.content: A member of an Antarctic expedition who allegedly threatened colleagues with a bladed weapon has been removed from a Korean research station in an emergency evacuation.

image.altText: A screenshot of CCTV vision showing a man in a blue and black jacket wielding a knife, with his face blurred.

items.title: Entire university sector accused of antisemitism failure

children.content: The special envoy on antisemitism has found not a single Australian university has met its requirements for adopting a definition of antisemitism.

items.title: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was

items.title: VAR drama, pitch invasion as Scottish league heads for final day shootout

items.title: Why Gold Coast has already won the NRL's Magic Round

items.title: MPs join calls for mayor in Melbourne to resign over character reference saga

items.title: King opens UK parliament but doomed PM Starmer may not last the week

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister remains on life support amid mounting speculation he could face a leadership challenge as early as Thursday, local time.
